content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1 / 5.6.3 WARN — RatingGateView.swift gates the App Store review link behind a self-built star selector: users tapping 4-5 stars are routed to `itms-apps://...write-review` while lower ratings are diverted to a feedback URL, bypassing `SKStoreReviewController`/`requestReview` entirely (App/RatingGateView.swift:14-21), reinforced by metadata's "rate us 5 stars!" call-to-action (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple requires the native review prompt so all users have an equal, unfiltered chance to rate — pre-filtering by star selection to only send happy users to the App Store is a classic 5.6.1 dark pattern and also risks a 5.6.3 discovery-fraud read. Replace this custom gate with `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview(in:)` (or `RequestReviewAction` in SwiftUI) triggered at a neutral point in the user journey, and drop the "rate us 5 stars" language from the description since it solicits a specific rating rather than honest feedback.
